$comment: c76 (D-ML7-2.3 move-cycle resolution + its scratch-picker soundness completion; 2026-07-01): an arg-register permutation at a call is resolved as a parallel copy whose cycle is broken via a config-driven caller-saved scratch of the right class that avoids EVERY committed/identity destination. WITNESS: an 8-FP-arg rotation (rot8) -> a single 8-cycle in xmm0..7 on SysV; sink positional-weights all 8 args so any mis-rotation changes the result. Rotated call passes (2,3,4,5,6,7,8,1) -> 2*1+3*2+4*3+5*4+6*5+7*6+8*7+1*8 = 176; main maps 176 -> 42. FP args are derived from a VOLATILE global (`one`==1) so they are non-constant -> the release arm does not trip the separate pre-existing FP-constant bug D-OPT-RELEASE-FP-CONSTANT-UNLOADED-XMM (c77); values equal constant rot8. RED-ON-DISABLE (two tiers): revert the whole move-cycle fix -> error[L_MoveCycleUnsupported] (pre-fix detector bails); revert only the scratch-picker soundness half -> the cycle-break scratch lands on a committed low arg register (xmm0) and clobbers a delivered arg -> a SILENT wrong value on SysV (rot8 -> 1). => 42.
